protection_settings.set_unit: × In bias slope (50%, high-impedance scheme), pickup Vs = 200 V across relay coil
× In bias slope (50%, high-impedance scheme), pickup Vs = 200 V across relay coil
s breaker failure time, current-check pickup 100 A primary (8.3% In)
s breaker failure time, current-check pickup 100 A primary (8.3% In)

assumptions: High-impedance busbar differential scheme assumed (vs low-impedance numerical 87B). High-impedance scheme is the dominant choice for 2-zone medium-voltage substation busbars because it requires lower CT performance class and simpler relay logic; numerical 87B (ABB REB670, Siemens 7SS85) is favoured on highly subdivided busbars with many zones.

Bias slope 50% selected because high-impedance schemes use a higher slope than biased differential — the pickup is voltage-based (Vs across the relay coil) rather than current-bias-based, and 50% is the typical operating-region setting that gives stability against the worst-case through-fault CT mismatch.

Stabilising resistor 2200 ohm sized per Vs = If × (Rct + 2 × Rl) where Vs = 200 V (relay knee), If = maximum through-fault CT secondary = 131 A (= 31.5 kA / 1200 × 5), Rct = 4 ohm (CT winding), Rl = 1.5 ohm (lead loop) → Vs = 131 × (4 + 3) = 917 V; 2200 ohm resistor designed to dissipate the through-fault voltage without operating the relay.

50BF breaker failure time 200 ms selected per IEC 60255-187 typical: 90 ms breaker operating time + 60 ms 50BF current-check element + 50 ms margin = 200 ms. Cascades to the zone lockout to trip the adjacent breakers if the primary breaker fails to clear.

Zone overlap at the bus-section: T4 (Zone 1 CT on the bus-section feeder) and T5 (Zone 2 CT on the bus-section feeder) are physically separate CT cores on the bus-section breaker (Q4/Q5 representing the same physical CB drawn as two items for zone-clarity on the schematic). Internal faults at the bus-section are detected by BOTH 87B-Z1 and 87B-Z2 — intentional overlap to ensure no dead-zone.

Leaf-mode execution: no db-layout-rollup intent (HV substation busbar sits upstream of any LV cascade), no fault-level intent (engineer-declared maximum through-fault 31.5 kA), no earthing intent (the busbar protection zone is independent of LV earthing system). The 31.5 kA through-fault assumption is the standard IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 reference for 33 kV switchgear breaking capacity.

ANSI code field representation: industry parlance uses '50BF' for breaker failure protection (ANSI 50 instantaneous overcurrent + 'BF' breaker-failure qualifier). The schematic IR schema regex ^[0-9]{1,3}[A-Z]?(T|B|N|G)?$ permits second-letter suffixes T/B/N/G only — so the field carries '50B' (50-Breaker-failure-element) while item.rating, label sequence_note text, and assumptions all retain the full '50BF' designation for clarity to the reading engineer.

rationale.chat_summary: 33 kV substation 2-zone busbar protection schematic (INT jurisdiction, IEC framework). High-impedance 87B (50% slope, Vs 200 V, 2200 ohm stabilising R) per zone plus 50BF breaker-failure (200 ms time, 100 A current-check) per breaker. Zone overlap at the bus-section via paired T4/T5 CTs. Leaf-mode execution — no consumed intents (busbar zone sits upstream of LV cascades). Reply to refine.

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: protection_busbar on sheet PROT-003 for a 33 kV INT substation with 2 zones, 4 feeders per zone (3 line + 1 bus-section), 8 total breakers, 8 dedicated protection CTs, 2 high-impedance 87B relays, 8 50BF breaker-failure relays, and 2 zone-master lockouts. INT jurisdiction applies the IEC framework directly: IEC 60255-187 (differential / busbar protection), IEC 60255-151 (instantaneous element on 50BF), IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 (CT performance, class PX for high-impedance), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 for graphical symbols.

decisions.label: High-impedance 87B scheme (not low-impedance numerical)
decisions.summary: High-impedance is the dominant choice for 2-zone medium-voltage substation busbars — simpler relay logic, lower CT performance demand, lower commissioning risk.
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60255-187 (differential / REF / busbar) + IEEE C37.234 (US busbar protection guide, cross-cited as the comprehensive design reference)

decisions.label: Leaf-mode execution
decisions.summary: No consumed intents — busbar zone sits upstream of any LV cascade; fault-level + earthing engineer-declared.
decisions.rule: Leaf-mode declaration per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: 33 kV three-phase substation busbar with two zones connected via a bus-section breaker. Engineer-declared maximum through-fault current 31.5 kA per IEC standard switchgear rating. Each feeder rated 1250 A (1200/5 CT primary covers up to ~1500 A overload). The bus-section breaker is rated 2000 A to handle worst-case load transfer between zones. No upstream intents available — this schematic sits at the top of the substation protection hierarchy and is the source for downstream cascades (which are sibling schematics, not consumed by this one).

decisions.label: Maximum through-fault 31.5 kA engineer-declared
decisions.summary: Standard IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 reference value for 33 kV switchgear breaking capacity at this current rating tier.

decisions.label: Zone overlap at bus-section
decisions.summary: T4 + T5 are independent CT cores on the bus-section breaker, one assigned to each zone — overlap ensures no dead-zone at the busbar boundary.
decisions.rule: Zone overlap discipline for busbar protection

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 28 devices placed: 8× CTs (T1-T8, all 1200/5 class PX for high-impedance scheme linearity), 2× differential relays (R87B-Z1 and R87B-Z2, one per zone), 8× breaker-failure relays (R50BF-Q1 through R50BF-Q8, one per breaker), 8× circuit breakers (Q1-Q8, 33 kV SF6 vacuum, 31.5 kA breaking), 2× zone lockouts (K86-Z1 and K86-Z2, hand-reset), plus three terminal blocks X1/X2 (CT summation buses per zone) and X3 (110 VDC trip circuit). Class PX is the canonical CT class for high-impedance differential schemes — IEC 60044-1 defines it as 'low-leakage-reactance CTs with specified knee-point voltage and excitation current' — and is required because the high-impedance scheme stability depends on linear CT response under through-fault conditions.

decisions.label: CT class PX (not 5P) for high-impedance scheme
decisions.summary: High-impedance schemes require CT linearity (PX class with specified knee-point voltage), not just accuracy at a specified ALF (5P class).
decisions.rule: CT class selection for high-impedance differential

decisions.label: 8× independent 50BF relays (one per breaker)
decisions.summary: One 50BF per breaker — failure of any breaker triggers the zone lockout, cascading to clear the busbar via the adjacent breakers.

sections.title: Protection Coordination
sections.summary: Two protection layers. Layer 1 (87B): high-impedance scheme, 50% slope, pickup Vs 200 V, 2200 ohm stabilising R, instantaneous (<30 ms). Detects faults inside the CT-bounded zone. Layer 2 (50BF): 8.3% In current-check + 200 ms delay; if current persists through the supposedly-tripped breaker, the zone lockout re-energises and clears all breakers in the zone. Zone overlap at the bus-section: T4 + T5 give dual coverage — internal faults at the bus-section trigger BOTH K86-Z1 and K86-Z2, clearing all 8 feeders (correct response for a bus-section fault).

decisions.label: 87B 50% slope, Vs 200 V, 2200 ohm stabilising R
decisions.summary: High-impedance scheme settings per IEC 60255-187 manufacturer commissioning guide.

decisions.label: 50BF time 200 ms = breaker + element + margin
decisions.summary: 90 ms typical 33 kV vacuum CB operating time + 60 ms 50BF current-check element + 50 ms margin = 200 ms.
decisions.code_clause: IEEE C37.234 § 5.5 (busbar protection breaker failure logic)

decisions.label: Zone overlap at bus-section
decisions.summary: T4 and T5 give dual coverage of the bus-section breaker — no dead-zone.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against IEC 60255-187 (differential/busbar), IEC 60255-151 (50BF instantaneous), IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 (CT performance class PX), IEC 61850-9-2 (process bus / GOOSE — applicable if the substation is digitalised; cited as the framework reference for future-proofing), IEEE C37.234 (US busbar protection guide, cross-cited as the comprehensive design reference), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (graphical symbols). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Six assumptions documented including the high-impedance scheme choice, stabilising-resistor sizing math, 50BF time derivation, zone-overlap discipline, and the leaf-mode declaration.

decisions.label: INT jurisdiction citation discipline
decisions.summary: IEC framework cited directly — no BS 7671 (not applicable on INT), no NEC (not applicable on INT). IEEE C37.234 cross-cited as the design-reference guide.

decisions.label: IEC 60044-1 class PX retained vs IEC 61869
decisions.summary: Class PX retained because manufacturer CT catalogues and substation panel-builder commissioning notes still predominantly use the IEC 60044-1 class PX nomenclature.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Leaf-mode execution explicit: no db-layout-rollup intent (the busbar zone sits upstream of any LV distribution-board cascade); no fault-level intent (the busbar IS the fault-level source for downstream cascades — its through-fault rating is engineer-declared at 31.5 kA per IEC standard switchgear breaking capacity); no earthing intent (the busbar protection zone is functionally independent of the LV earthing system). All upstream context engineer-declared via inputs.json. No tool_call_pending entries — all protection settings hand-verifiable against the high-impedance scheme design equations (Vs = If × (Rct + 2 × Rl)) and the 50BF time derivation.

decisions.label: Leaf-mode declared explicit
decisions.summary: No upstream intents; busbar is the source of downstream cascades, not a consumer.

decisions.label: No tool_call_pending entries
decisions.summary: All four protection_settings entries fully resolved at LLM-emission time.
decisions.rule: tool_call_pending discipline per generator prompt Step 8
